hypothesis: Integrating a differentiable long-range electrostatics component into the mlip framework—where per-atom partial charges are predicted by a lightweight neural network and used in a stable Ewald-like correction—will markedly improve energy and force predictions for charged solvated systems (e.g., SPICE2 charged subsets) by at least 15% MAE reduction and maintain competitive inference speed relative to purely local MLIP models.
